A.missing: corporation as an organization run by many people is unspecified; thing2 is bare
A.reason: Person owns a part (share) of some thing; captures part-of-a-thing ownership but no notion of corporation.

B.missing: no THINK/claim framing and thing3 (corporation) uncharacterized; owns a part of a thing
B.reason: Renders owning a part of a thing but the corporation genus is left as bare SOMETHING.

C.missing: corporation unspecified as a people-run entity; part-ownership present but genus thin
C.reason: Owns some part of a thing and thinks it is his; corporation not characterized.

D.missing: no part-of relation, no corporation; just owns a thing
D.reason: Merely someone who thinks a thing is his — fits any owner, not a shareholder.

E.missing: share as a formal part of a corporation; adds vague good-feeling bloat
E.reason: Big thing many people do, owns a small part — gestures at company but the criterial share-ownership is diluted by bloat.

F.missing: corporation as people-run entity unspecified; one part of a thing owned
F.reason: Owns one part of a thing and thinks it his; the corporation genus is bare SOMETHING.

G.missing: double IS-MINE muddles; corporation as thing many people do gestured but part-ownership overstated
G.reason: Thing many people do, owns a part of it; approaches the sense but the framing is muddled and corporation thinly rendered.
